title: crotalaria virgulata subsp. longistyla
intro: Crotalaria virgulata subsp. longistyla is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae, known for its distinctive yellow flowers and medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Crotalaria virgulata subsp. longistyla include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular natural remedy in many cultures. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be taken orally or applied topically to specific areas of the body.
